sections.summary: Scope: this standard applies to preserved tomato products (as defined in Section 2) offered for direct consumption, catering, repacking or indicated for further processing, but excludes dried tomatoes and preserved tomatoes containing other vegetables in quantities that materially alter the tomato component.

sections.section: Adopted in 1981. Revised in 2007. Amended in 2013, 2017, 2022, 2023.
sections.summary: Administrative history: the Standard for Preserved Tomatoes was adopted in 1981, revised in 2007, and amended in 2013, 2017, 2022 and 2023.

This standard applies to preserved tomato products as defined in Section 2 for direct consumption (including catering and repacking) and for products intended for further processing, excluding dried tomatoes and formulations with other vegetables that materially change the tomato's flavour, aroma or taste.

Description: defines preserved tomatoes as products from fresh, washed, ripe tomatoes processed by heat and packed with or without suitable packing medium; classifies varietal types by shape, details styles (whole/unwhole and subtypes like diced, sliced, wedges, pulp), allows other distinctive presentations if they meet requirements and are labelled, and begins to define pack types (e.g., solid pack).

Product definition: preserved tomatoes are made from fresh, washed, ripened red or reddish Lycopersicon/Lycopersicum esculentum fruit with stems/calices (and cores if needed) removed, packed with or without suitable packing medium/seasonings, and heat‑processed before or after hermetic sealing to prevent spoilage.
Varietal type: tomatoes may be designated by distinct varietal groups based on shape, e.g., round (globular/semi‑globular) or elongated types such as cylinder, pear, egg or plum.
Defines the 'Round' varietal type as having a globular or semi‑globular shape.

sections.section: 2.2.2 Cylinder, pear, egg or plum : elongated shape.
sections.summary: Defines cylindrical/pear/egg/plum varietal types as having an elongated shape.

Styles: preserved tomatoes may be whole (retain shape, usually peeled) or unwhole (pieces) with substyles such as diced, sliced, wedges, or pulp, and other distinctive presentations are permitted if they meet all relevant requirements and are adequately labelled to avoid consumer confusion.

sections.section: 2.3.1 Whole : Tomatoes which keep their initial shape after processing.
sections.summary: Defines the 'Whole' presentation as preserved tomatoes that retain their initial shape after processing.

sections.section: 2.3.2 Unwhole (Pieces): Tomatoes crushed or cut into sections whose shape may be irregular or regular.
sections.summary: Defines the 'Unwhole (Pieces)' presentation as preserved tomatoes crushed or cut into sections whose shape may be regular or irregular.

Permits other presentations of preserved tomatoes provided they are clearly distinctive from standard styles, comply with all relevant standard requirements (including defect limits and drained weight analogous to the closest style), and are labelled adequately to avoid misleading consumers.
Heading for 'Types of pack' that introduces packaging categories and notes the 'solid pack' subtype (no added packing medium).

sections.section: 2.4.1 Solid pack - without any added packing medium.
sections.summary: Defines 'Solid pack' as preserved tomatoes packed without any added packing medium.

sections.section: 2.4.2 Regular pack - with a packing medium added, as specified in Section 3.1.2.
sections.summary: Defines 'Regular pack' as preserved tomatoes packed with a packing medium, with the allowable media specified in Section 3.1.2.

Sets essential composition (tomatoes and permitted packing media and ingredients), quality criteria (normal colour, flavour, odour), definitions of defects, and numeric limits; and establishes lot acceptance rules. It permits specific packing media and ingredients (spices, salt, sugars when acidifying), defines whole‑style composition and defect limits (e.g., whole style drained minimums, peel and blemish area limits, pH ≤4.5), and requires lot acceptance based on an AQL 6.5 sampling plan and national mould‑count limits.

Specifies composition of preserved tomatoes as tomatoes plus an appropriate packing medium (see 3.1.2), lists permitted packing media (juice, puree/concentrate, pulp, water with restriction), and allows certain ingredients (spices/herbs, salt, and sugars when acidifying with specific labelling).
States the basic ingredients are tomatoes (as defined in Section 2) and, where appropriate, a packing medium as defined in Section 3.1.2.

Lists permitted packing media for preserved tomatoes: (a) juice (from ripe tomatoes, canning residue, or diluted concentrate), (b) tomato puree or concentrate (per CXS 57‑1981), (c) pulp (skinless ground tomatoes), and (d) water (only for unpeeled preserved tomatoes).

Lists permitted packing media for preserved tomatoes: (a) juice (from ripe tomatoes, canning residue, or diluted concentrate), (b) tomato puree or concentrate (per CXS 57‑1981), (c) pulp (skinless ground tomatoes), and (d) water (only for unpeeled preserved tomatoes).

Lists permitted packing media for preserved tomatoes: (a) juice (from ripe tomatoes, canning residue, or diluted concentrate), (b) tomato puree or concentrate (per CXS 57‑1981), (c) pulp (skinless ground tomatoes), and (d) water (only for unpeeled preserved tomatoes).

Lists permitted packing media for preserved tomatoes: (a) juice (from ripe tomatoes, canning residue, or diluted concentrate), (b) tomato puree or concentrate (per CXS 57‑1981), (c) pulp (skinless ground tomatoes), and (d) water (only for unpeeled preserved tomatoes).

Permits other ingredients in preserved tomatoes including spices and aromatic herbs (excluding tomato flavouring), salt (per the Food Grade Salt standard CXS 150‑1985), and sugars (per CXS 212‑1999) when acidifying agents are used, with specific labelling required for sugars.

sections.section: 3.1.3.1 a) spices, aromatic herbs (such as basil leaves) and natural extracts of these and seasonings excluding tomato flavouring
sections.summary: Permits spices, aromatic herbs (e.g., basil) and their natural extracts and seasonings to be used in preserved tomatoes, excluding tomato flavouring.

sections.section: 3.1.3.2 b) salt (sodium chloride) in accordance with the Standard for Food Grade Salt (CXS 150-1985)
sections.summary: Allows addition of salt (sodium chloride) to preserved tomatoes in accordance with the Standard for Food Grade Salt (CXS 150‑1985).

sections.section: 3.1.3.3 c) when acidifying agents are used, sugars, as listed in the Standard for Sugars (CXS 212-1999) with specific labelling
sections.summary: Permits sugars listed in the Standard for Sugars (CXS 212‑1999) to be used in preserved tomatoes when acidifying agents are applied, provided specific labelling is used.

Requires preserved tomatoes to have normal colour, flavour and odour (including for products with added ingredients) and defines defects and tolerances; it sets composition/wholeness rules (whole style ≥65% drained whole or almost whole; small cans ≥70%) and specific defect limits (whole peeled peel ≤30 cm²/kg, blemishes ≤3.5 cm²/kg), stipulates pH ≤4.5, and defers mould‑count limits to national legislation.

sections.section: 3.2.1 Definitions of defects and other terms used in Section 3.2
sections.summary: Provides definitions used in quality criteria, including 'whole or almost whole', 'objectionable core material', 'blemishes', 'extraneous plant material', and 'peel' (residual skin pieces >5 mm).

sections.section: 3.2.1.1 Whole or almost whole: a tomato of any size in which the contour is not materially altered by coring or trimming; the unit may be readily restored to practically its original conformation; it may be slightly cracked or split but not to the extent that there is substantial loss of shape.
sections.summary: Defines 'Whole or almost whole' as a tomato whose contour is not materially altered by coring or trimming, readily restorable to its original shape, and only slightly cracked or split without substantial loss of shape.

sections.section: 3.2.1.2 Objectionable core material : tough and fibrous texture or tomato tissue representing the tomato core that is definitely objectionable as to appearance and edibility.
sections.summary: Defines 'objectionable core material' in tomatoes as tough, fibrous texture or core tissue that is clearly objectionable in appearance and edibility.

sections.section: 3.2.1.3 Blemishes : areas into which lesions on the surface have penetrated and as a result thereof contrast strongly in colour or texture with the normal tomato tissue and should normally have been removed during processing.
sections.summary: Defines 'blemishes' as surface lesions that have penetrated and contrast strongly in colour or texture with normal tomato tissue and which should normally have been removed during processing.

sections.section: 3.2.1.4 Extraneous plant material : tomato leaves, stems, calyx bracts, and similar plant material.
sections.summary: Defines 'extraneous plant material' for preserved tomatoes as tomato leaves, stems, calyx bracts and similar plant parts.

sections.section: 3.2.1.5 Peel (or skin) : the residual pieces of skin, having a length higher than 5 mm, which adheres to the tomato flesh or is found loose in the container.
sections.summary: Defines 'peel (or skin)' as residual pieces of tomato skin longer than 5 mm that adhere to the flesh or are found loose in the container.

States that size/wholeness is only relevant to the 'whole' style and requires preserved tomatoes in that style to contain at least 65% m/m drained whole or almost whole units (allowing one non-whole unit per container), with a 70% requirement for small cans (<500 g).

sections.section: 3.2.2.1 Size or wholeness, as such, is only a factor in the style designated as 'whole' style . Preserved tomatoes of 'whole' style shall consist of not less than 65 percent m/m of drained tomatoes in whole or almost whole units, except that in any container there may be one unit that is not whole. For small cans (less than 500 g net weight) this value shall be 70 percent.
sections.summary: Specifies for 'whole' style preserved tomatoes a minimum of 65% m/m drained whole or almost whole units (one allowed non-whole unit per container) and 70% for small cans (<500 g).

Requires the finished product to be substantially free from objectionable core material and extraneous plant material and not to exceed specified defect limits (e.g., peel and blemish area limits), with mould count set by national law and pH not exceeding 4.5.

sections.section: 3.2.3.1 Peel ( only for whole and peeled styles):
sections.summary: For whole and peeled styles, limits aggregate peel area to not more than 30 cm2 per kg of total contents for whole peeled product.

sections.section: 3.2.3.1.1 Whole peeled: not more than 30 cm 2  aggregate area per kg of total contents.
sections.summary: Specifies that whole peeled tomatoes shall have no more than 30 cm2 aggregate peel area per kg of total contents.
Limits aggregate blemish area in preserved tomatoes to not more than 3.5 cm2 per kg of total contents.
States that mould count limits for preserved tomatoes shall be set according to the legislation of the country of retail sale.
Sets a maximum pH of 4.5 for preserved tomatoes.
Declares any container that fails to meet one or more applicable quality requirements in Section 3.2 to be a 'defective'.

Specifies lot acceptance criteria: a lot meets quality requirements when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number c) of the appropriate sampling plan at an AQL of 6.5 and the mould-count allowance is not exceeded, with these criteria not applying to non-retail containers.
Permits the use of firming agents and certain other food additives listed in Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in foods conforming to this standard.

Requires compliance with the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins (CXS 193-1995) and Codex pesticide MRLs, noting that determination of maximum levels should account for product concentration using natural total soluble solids with a reference value of 4.5 for fresh fruit.

Recommends preparing and handling products in accordance with Codex hygiene texts (General Principles of Food Hygiene, Code for Low and Acidified Low-Acid Canned Foods, etc.) and complying with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

Sets weights-and-measures rules: containers must be filled to at least 90% of water capacity (minus necessary headspace), drained weight should be at least 50% of container water capacity, noncompliance with fill is a 'defective', lot acceptance follows sampling plans with AQL 6.5, and these provisions do not apply to non-retail containers.

Requires containers to be well filled with product occupying not less than 90% of water capacity (minus necessary headspace), treats failure to meet minimum fill as a 'defective', and sets lot acceptance and minimum drained weight requirements with AQL 6.5; provisions exclude non-retail containers.

Specifies minimum fill: the sealed container (including packing medium) must occupy not less than 90% of its water capacity (volume of distilled water at 20°C the sealed container will hold), minus any necessary headspace per good manufacturing practice.
A container that does not meet the minimum fill requirement in Section 7.1.1 is classified as a 'defective'.
A lot meets the requirement of Section 7.1.1 when the number of 'defectives' (per Section 7.1.2) does not exceed the acceptance number c) of the applicable sampling plan based on an AQL of 6.5.

The drained weight of the product must be at least 50% (calculated on the weight of distilled water at 20°C that the sealed container holds when completely filled). Lot acceptance is met when the average drained weight of all containers examined is not less than the required minimum, provided there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.

sections.section: 7.1.4.1 The drained weight of the product should be not less than 50 percent, calculated on the basis of the weight of distilled water at 20  o C which the sealed container will hold when completely filled. iv
sections.summary: The drained weight of the product must be not less than 50%, calculated on the basis of the weight of distilled water at 20°C that the sealed container will hold when completely filled.

Minimum drained-weight requirements are deemed complied with when the average drained weight of all containers examined is at least the required minimum, provided there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.

Heading and introductory provision: products covered by this standard must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), with additional specific labelling provisions set out in the following clauses.

sections.section: 8.1 The  product  covered  by  the  provisions  of  this  standard  shall  be  labelled  in  accordance  with  the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985). 9  In addition, the following specific provisions apply:
sections.summary: The product must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), with the additional specific provisions in this standard applying.

Name and labelling requirements for the product: the name must reflect presentation ('peeled/whole peeled tomatoes' for peeled whole product, 'tomatoes' otherwise, 'unpeeled tomatoes' if peel not removed or allowances in 3.2.3.1 are not respected); styles (Section 2.3) and packing media (Section 3.1.2) must be declared near the name; added ingredients that alter flavour must be indicated as 'flavoured with X' or 'X flavoured'; other styles must carry additional wording to avoid misleading consumers; and optional statements such as type ('solid pack') and packing material ('juice' etc.) may be shown if criteria are met.

sections.section: 8.2.1 The name of the product shall be:
sections.summary: Heading only: introduces the specific product name requirements that follow in subsequent clauses.

sections.section: 8.2.2 The styles, as defined in Section 2.3 and the packing media defined in Section 3.1.2 shall be declared as part of the name or in close proximity to the name.
sections.summary: Styles (as defined in Section 2.3) and the packing media (Section 3.1.2) must be declared as part of, or in close proximity to, the product name.

sections.section: 8.2.3 If an added ingredient, as defined in Section 3.1.3, alters the flavour characteristic of the product, the name of the food shall be accompanied by the term 'flavoured with X' or 'X flavoured' as appropriate.
sections.summary: If an added ingredient (Section 3.1.3) alters the product's characteristic flavour, the name must be accompanied by the term 'flavoured with X' or 'X flavoured' as appropriate.

sections.section: 8.2.4 Other styles - If the product is produced in accordance with the other styles provision (Section 2.3.3), the label should contain in close proximity to the name of the product such additional words or phrases that will avoid misleading or confusing the consumer.
sections.summary: When the product is produced under the 'other styles' provision (Section 2.3.3), the label must include additional words or phrases near the product name to avoid misleading or confusing consumers.

sections.section: 8.2.5 The following may be stated on the label:
sections.summary: Optional label statements permitted: a) the type 'solid pack' if the pack complies with Section 2.4.1; and b) the packing material (e.g., 'juice') if the pack complies with Section 2.4.2.

sections.section:
8.2.5 The following may be stated on the label:
- a) the type : 'solid pack' if the pack complies with Section 2.4.1;
sections.summary: 'Solid pack' may be stated on the label to indicate type when the pack complies with Section 2.4.1.

sections.section:
8.2.5 The following may be stated on the label:
- b) the packing material : 'juice' or other, if the pack complies with Section 2.4.2.
sections.summary: The packing material (for example 'juice') may be stated on the label if the pack complies with Section 2.4.2.
Labelling of non-retail containers must follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.section: 8.3.1 The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021). 10
sections.summary: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
For compliance checking with this standard, use the methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) that are relevant to the standard's provisions.
